sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports intermittent right groin pain for over two years, worsening with movement and pivoting, and rates the pain 4-6 out of 10. He states the pain started after walking around an infusion room and has now spread to all activities. He reports a recent weight loss of 110 pounds through weight watchers and denies fever or chills. He denies bowel or bladder changes.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: Physical exam reveals decreased range of motion in the right hip with pain on internal and external rotation and resisted hip flexion, with pain reported at 3/7. Neurological exam is normal. Vital signs are stable. No ankle edema is noted.
medical_concepts: pain on internal rotation of hip
pain on external rotation of hip

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The primary problem is right hip pain, likely secondary to degenerative joint disease, as suggested by prior imaging.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: Rescribe NSAIDs as needed for pain relief. Discuss a low-impact exercise program and a cane for offloading. Schedule a follow-up appointment in three months to assess response to treatment and consider a cortisone injection into the right hip joint.

medical_concepts: pain on internal rotation of hip
pain on external rotation of hip
